title: Sara and Eva Meet a Baby Deer
logline: Sara spots two baby deer in the backyard. She whispers Eva over to the kitchen window, and together — with Mama, Ginger, and Joe — the family learns how to meet wild animals the gentle way.
learning_moment: Being gentle and quiet with wild animals; patience; wonder at nature.
tone: Warm, quiet-magical, a Pixar-tier wonder moment that kids want to rewatch.

pages.beat: COLD OPEN — warm narrator sets the scene over an aerial establishing shot of the property. Camera slowly pushes down toward the kitchen window.

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: In a quiet suburban neighborhood, on a bright spring morning, in a cozy stone house with a rainbow afghan on the couch and a rainbow of kids' drawings on the fridge...

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: ...lived two sisters. A big one, named Sara. And a little one, named Eva. And on this particular morning...

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: ...something very small, and very new, and very spotted, had come into their backyard.

pages.beat: HOOK — Sara at the kitchen window, breath fogs the glass. She goes wide-eyed. Whisper-calls Eva.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: (whispers) Eva... Eva, come here. Come here right now.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: And walk very, very quietly.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (bouncing in from the living room) Whaaat? Why do I have to be quiet? I'm never quiet!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Shhhh. Look. Through the window. On the grass. By the big tree.

segments.note: Eva pads over, climbs the kitchen stool, looks out.

pages.beat: Eva sees them. Big wow. First full reveal of the two fawns in the yard (camera finally shows them from the girls' POV through the window).

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (soft gasp) ...Sara.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Really?! Really-really?!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Really-really. Two of them. Right by the big tree.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (whisper-squeal) They're so tiiiiiinyyyy.

segments.note: Hold on the window reflection — both sisters' wide eyes, fawns in soft focus beyond.

pages.beat: Mama comes in drying her hands, crouches between them. Explains what they are.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: What are you two staring at? ...oh.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: Oh, wow. Girls.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: Those are baby deer. They're called fawns.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Fawns? Like the word for a baby deer?

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: Exactly. And see the little spots on their backs? Those help them hide in the forest, so nothing can find them. That's their superpower.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (whispers) Spots are a superpower? Whoa.

pages.beat: Ginger pads in, freezes, ears go up. She's about to bark. Sara lunges softly to stop her.

segments.speaker: Ginger
segments.text: *Ears up!*

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: (very soft) No, Ginger. Not today. Shhhh. Good girl.

segments.speaker: Ginger
segments.text: (low confused whine)

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: She knows something's out there, doesn't she. Good girl, Ginger. Quiet paws. Quiet paws.

segments.note: Ginger sits, tense, ears swiveling. Sara gently rests a hand on her back.

pages.beat: Joe comes bouncing in, ready to bark at the window. Eva scoops him up fast.

segments.speaker: Joe
segments.text: Spin, spin, spin! (little happy yip)

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: No no no no no, Zhorik, not now! Come here, fluffy buddy.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Shhhhh. Shhhhh.

segments.speaker: Joe
segments.text: (muffled tiny whimper into Eva's sweater)

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Good catch, Eva.

pages.beat: They tiptoe from the kitchen, through the living room, toward the sliding door to the backyard. Sara gives the plan.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: If we want to see them closer, we have to be sloooow. No running. No talking. Just watching.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (whisper) Like a ninja?

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Like a gentle ninja.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Follow me. Wait — I have a plan.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: I'm listening, detective.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Step one. We open the door just a crack. Step two. We sit on the patio. Step three — this is the most important step — we do not move. We do not breathe loud.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (whisper-excited) I love steps.

segments.note: The family tip-toes in a gentle single-file line across the wood floor, Eva holding Joe like a football.

pages.beat: Outside on the patio. The fawns see them. Time stops. A held-breath moment — Pixar peak. MOSTLY SILENT.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: (barely above a whisper) Freeze, girls. They saw us.

segments.note: Slow push-in on the fawns' wide, wet, curious eyes. Birds chirp softly. Wind moves leaves.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (tiny) I'm frozen, I'm frozen, I'm frozen...

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Breathe slooow. In. Out.

segments.note: Close-up: Eva's exaggerated slow belly breath. Sara's gentle smile watching her.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (soft) Ohhh. Their ears are so big.

pages.beat: One of the fawns takes a curious step toward them. Eva almost bursts.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (tiny-but-huge) Sara. Sara. Sara-Sara-Sara. It's walking closer!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Don't move. Don't move.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Really?! Really-really?!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Really-really. Just stay soft.

segments.note: The fawn takes two more tentative steps into the morning sun. Light catches its coat.

pages.beat: Ginger, hero dog, holds her silence. Joe is tucked quiet in Eva's arms. Fawn a few steps away.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Ginger, you're being so brave right now.

segments.speaker: Ginger
segments.text: (proud tiny huff)

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: Look at how close he's getting. Oh, girls...

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (whisper) Hi, little deer. Hi. I'm Eva.

segments.note: Fawn tilts its head. Eva tilts her head the same way, unconsciously.

pages.beat: A crow caws from a tree. Both fawns startle and leap away gracefully over the back fence. Slow-motion beat.

segments.note: Sharp crow CAW. Ginger's ears flick.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Oh!

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: They're flying! Are they flying?!

segments.note: SLOW-MOTION: both fawns arc gracefully over the iron fence, white tails up, into the trees. Golden dust motes catch light.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: (laughing softly) Leaping, sweetheart. Deer jump like that.

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Wow. Wow wow wow.

pages.beat: Resolution on the patio. Warm close-up. Mama delivers the gentle lesson.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Really?! Really-really?! That happened? In OUR yard?!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: In our yard. And we were quiet enough to see them.

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: That's how you meet wild friends, girls. Gentle and slow. Not grabbing. Not chasing. Just watching.

segments.speaker: Joe
segments.text: Spin, spin, spin!

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: (giggling) Now you can spin, Zhorik. Now you can spin.

segments.note: Joe does a tiny happy twirl on the patio. Everyone laughs quietly. Warm close-up family shot.

pages.beat: TIME CUT — that evening. Papa is home from work. Dinner at the dining room table. The girls retell the story in overlapping excited voices.

segments.speaker: Papa
segments.text: So. I hear there was a visitor today.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Papa. Papa. Papa-Papa-Papa. There were TWO visitors. Baby deer. WITH spots!

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: Fawns. They're called fawns. And the spots are a superpower, Papa. They HIDE them.

segments.speaker: Papa
segments.text: A superpower? In our yard? This I've got to hear. Start from the beginning.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: Okay. Okay okay okay. I was in the living room. And Sara was at the window. And she said—

segments.speaker: Sara
segments.text: (grinning) Eva, come here, come here right now.

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: —and I came here, and there were TWO BABIES ON THE GRASS.

pages.beat: Papa's warm reaction. Small beat where Ginger gets a pat for being 'the quietest dog in the world today' and Joe gets a little piece of carrot for being carried like a good boy.

segments.speaker: Papa
segments.text: Ginger. Is this true? You didn't bark?

segments.speaker: Ginger
segments.text: (proud-dog chuff)

segments.speaker: Mama
segments.text: Not one single bark. Not one. She was a legend.

segments.speaker: Papa
segments.text: And you, little Zhorik? You were a legend too?

segments.speaker: Joe
segments.text: (tiny proud yip) Spin, spin, spin!

segments.speaker: Eva
segments.text: He was QUIET, Papa. I held him like a loaf of bread.

segments.speaker: Papa
segments.text: (laughing) A loaf of bread! Okay. Legendary family all around.

pages.beat: OUTRO — warm narrator wrap-up, camera pulls back to aerial of the house as the sun sets.

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: And that — is how Sara and Eva met their very first wild friends. Gentle and slow. Quiet and soft. And sometimes...

segments.speaker: Narrator
segments.text: ...if you're patient enough, the whole world tiptoes right up to your back door.

segments.note: Camera pulls back from the dining room window, up and over the house, sun setting on the neighborhood. Title card: 'Sara & Eva — Episode 1'.
